_comment: spec-v593 section 2 exception class: metric that IS US trade practice. Each entry waives check-us-defaults findings whose label contains `match` in `module` (module "*" = any). Additions are reviewed like citations, against the section-2 acid test: would a US tradesperson read this number in this unit off their own instrument, code table, or product label?

entries.justification: mg/L is the universal US water-ops unit (SDWA/SWTR reporting, jar tests, MLSS).
SV30 settleometer reads mL/L on the cone itself.
Sludge Volume Index (SVI) is universally reported in mL/g in US wastewater ops (Standard Methods, WEF, operator-certification exams); there is no US-customary equivalent.
CT tables (SWTR) are published in mg-min/L.
Conductivity meters read uS/cm at 25 C.
Span deflection ratio L/x (L = span), not liters.
Peak ground / seismic acceleration in g (gravity), not grams: ASCE 7 and USGS publish in g.
entries.note: grams collisions are covered by module-scoped lab/kitchen entries

entries.justification: NEC 310/table ambients and conductor ratings are published in deg C columns.
ICEA P-32-382 conductor short-circuit limits are published in deg C.
US lab practice is SI throughout: mL, uL, mg, g/mol, M, g/mL, cm cuvette paths, mm rotors.
Baker's percentage and equilibrium-cure recipes are written in grams in US kitchens.
UTM easting/northing are defined in meters (USGS quads print meters).
UTM easting/northing are defined in meters.
The World Magnetic Model takes altitude in km.
TIA-568 defines the 90 m permanent link / 100 m channel in meters.
TIA-568 permanent link is specified in meters.
TIA-568 cord budgets are specified in meters.
Fiber attenuation is specified in dB/km on every datasheet.
TIA-568 temperature derating tables are published in deg C.
Camera sensor formats and lens focal lengths are sold in mm.
Lens focal lengths are sold in mm.
Metric thread pitch is the product spec on the box.
Metric thread pitch is the product spec on the box.
The tile's metric-thread entry path; US pitch field sits beside it.
Brake pad thickness is published and measured in mm in US shops.
Pad wear-rate shop data rides the mm pad-thickness convention.
Wheel offset is stamped ET in mm on the wheel itself.
LED panel pixel pitch is sold in mm.
Module datasheets publish record temps and coefficients in deg C.
Cell/ambient temperature model inputs are datasheet deg C.
NOCT is a datasheet deg C value.
Power temperature coefficients are datasheet %/deg C.
Peak-sun-hours insolation data is published in kWh/m2/day (NREL).
Plane-of-array irradiance is published in W/m^2.
Spore-trap lab reports count spores per cubic meter.
Air-sampling pumps are calibrated in L/min.

SCBA breathing/consumption rate is expressed in L/min: NIOSH rates open-circuit SCBA duration at 40 L/min and NFPA 1981 heavy-work testing at ~103 L/min, and US fireground air-management training teaches ~40 L/min light / ~100 L/min heavy. There is no US-customary breathing-rate unit a firefighter reads off the rating.
Sampling cassette volumes are specified in liters.
Carbon filter adsorption capacity is published in grams on the product sheet.
Soil particle density is quoted in g/cc (2.65) in US soils practice and every geotech lab report.
ACI 318 minimum-thickness span ratios l/20..l/28 (l = span), not liters.
Span ratio result line (l = span), not liters.
IEEE Std 80 step/touch equations take the crushed-rock layer hs in meters as published.
US beverage bottles are labeled in mL (750 mL).
Loan Estimate section C (services you can shop for), not Celsius.
NEC subsection letter in a code citation, not Celsius.
SWTR CT tables are published against deg C water temperature.
lightning-countdown secondary metric output line; the primary Distance (mi) output line precedes it -- the sanctioned US-primary + metric-secondary pattern.
